 960→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 961→        node: ASTNode,
 962→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 963→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 964→    ) {
 965→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 966→            return
 967→        }
 968→
 969→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 970→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 971→        val text = node.text
 972→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 973→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 974→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 975→                emit(
 976→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 977→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 978→                    true,
 979→                )
 980→            } else {
 981→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 982→                // been emitted.
 983→            }
 984→            LOGGER.trace {
 985→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 986→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 987→            }
 988→            if (autoCorrect) {
 989→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 990→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 991→                )
 992→            }
 993→        } else {
 994→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 995→        }
 996→    }

 998→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 999→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1000→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1001→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1002→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1003→        }
1004→
1005→        nextLeaf
1006→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1007→            ?.let { comment ->
1008→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1009→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1010→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1011→                }
1012→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1013→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1014→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1015→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1016→                    return true
1017→                }
1018→            }
1019→        return false

1027→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1028→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1029→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1030→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1031→            when {
1032→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1033→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1034→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1035→
1036→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1037→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1038→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1039→
1040→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1041→            }
1042→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1043→    }

1045→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1046→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1047→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1048→            SPACE -> {
1049→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1050→                    emit(
1051→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1052→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1053→                        true,
1054→                    )
1055→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1056→                } else {
1057→                    nodeIndent
1058→                }
1059→            }
1060→
1061→            TAB -> {
1062→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1063→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1064→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1065→                    emit(
1066→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1067→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1068→                        true,
1069→                    )
1070→                    indentConfig
1071→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1072→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1073→                } else {
1074→                    nodeIndent
1075→                }
1076→            }
1077→        }
1078→    }

1096→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {

1098→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1099→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1100→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1101→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1102→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1103→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1104→                }
1105→
1106→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1107→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1108→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1109→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1110→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1111→                }
1112→
1113→                else -> ""
1114→            }
1115→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1116→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1117→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1118→        } else {
1119→            ""

1121→    }

1139→    private data class IndentContext(
1140→        /**
1141→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1142→         */
1143→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1144→        /**
1145→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1146→         * on which the indent context starts.
1147→         */
1148→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1149→        /**
1150→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1151→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1152→         */
1153→        val nodeIndent: String,
1154→        /**
1155→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1156→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1157→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1158→         */
1159→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1160→        /**
1161→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1162→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1163→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1164→         */
1165→        val childIndent: String,
1166→        /**
1167→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1168→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1169→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1170→         */
1171→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1172→        /**
1173→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1174→         */
1175→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1176→    ) {
1177→        val nodes: String
1178→            get() =
1179→                fromASTNode
1180→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1181→                    ?.leaves()
1182→                    .orEmpty()
1183→                    .takeWhile {
1184→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1185→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1186→                    }
1187→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1188→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1189→
1190→        fun indent() =
1191→            if (activated) {
1192→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1193→            } else {
1194→                nodeIndent
1195→            }
1196→
1197→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1198→    }
1199→}

1203→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1204→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1205→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1206→            Pair("[", "]")
1207→        } else {
1208→            Pair("", "")
1209→        }
1210→    return prefix
1211→        .plus(
1212→            this
1213→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1214→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1215→        ).plus(suffix)
1216→}

1218→private class StringTemplateIndenter(private val indentConfig: IndentConfig) {
1219→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1220→        expectedIndent: String,
1221→        node: ASTNode,
1222→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1223→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1224→    ) {
1225→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1226→        node
1227→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1228→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1229→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1230→            ?.let { _ ->
1231→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1232→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1233→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1234→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1235→                    emit(
1236→                        node.startOffset,
1237→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1238→                        false,
1239→                    )
1240→                    return
1241→                }
1242→
1243→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1244→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1245→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1246→                    if (node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1247→                        // Allow:
1248→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1249→                        //       return """
1250→                        //           some text
1251→                        //           """.trimIndent
1252→                        //   }
1253→                        node
1254→                            .indent(false)
1255→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1256→                    } else if (node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1257→                        // Allow:
1258→                        //   fun foo(
1259→                        //       bar: String
1260→                        //   ) = """
1261→                        //       $bar
1262→                        //       """.trimIndent
1263→                        node
1264→                            .indent(false)
1265→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1266→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1267→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1268→                        // should have no indent as well.
1269→                        ""
1270→                    } else {
1271→                        expectedIndent
1272→                    }
1273→                node.children()
1274→                    .forEach {
1275→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1276→                            (
1277→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1278→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1279→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1280→                                )
1281→                        ) {
1282→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1283→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1284→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1285→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1286→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1287→                                } else {
1288→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1289→                                }
1290→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1291→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1292→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1293→                                emit(
1294→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1295→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1296→                                        "string",
1297→                                    true,
1298→                                )

411→public fun ASTNode.leavesIncludingSelf(forward: Boolean = true): Sequence<ASTNode> {
412→    val sequence =
413→        if (isLeaf()) {
414→            sequenceOf(this)
415→        } else {
416→            emptySequence()
417→        }
418→    return sequence.plus(leaves(forward))
419→}

1→# Changelog

  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased

  7→### Added

 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 13→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 14→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 15→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 16→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 17→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 18→
 19→### Changed
 20→* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers

 22→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 23→
 24→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 25→
 26→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 27→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 28→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 29→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 30→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 31→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 32→
 33→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 34→
 35→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 36→
 37→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 38→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 39→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 40→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 41→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 42→
 43→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 44→
 45→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 46→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 47→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 48→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 49→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 50→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 51→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 52→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 53→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 54→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 55→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 56→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 57→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 58→
 59→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 60→
 61→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 62→
 63→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 64→
 65→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 66→
 67→#### Experimental rules
 68→
 69→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 70→
 71→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 72→
 73→#### EditorConfig
 74→
 75→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 76→
 77→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 78→
 79→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 80→
 81→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 82→
 83→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 84→
 85→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 86→
 87→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 88→
 89→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 90→
 91→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 92→
 93→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 94→
 95→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
